verdict.answer_first: B states the plan sooner: “Turn on automated backups today” and “Take a manual snapshot right now”; A opens with framing and buries the immediate move until “What I'd do right now.”
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to the highest-stakes path: “production Aurora PostgreSQL... do this first”; A surveys many stores and ends by asking “Which database did you actually mean.”
verdict.fabrication: A invents more context: “revenue/users/UTM,” “Supabase (ads),” “Neo4j knowledge graph,” “PostHog”; B also assumes “production Aurora PostgreSQL” and “local on this Mac,” but less expansively.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it gives a short priority order: “Turn on automated backups,” “Take a manual snapshot,” “Set a recovery target,” “Add a logical dump,” “Test a restore.”
